description_long: This object is a dual-sided traffic light mounted on a tall, black metal pole with a pointed finial at the top and a sturdy, round concrete base. Each side features a set of three vertically aligned signal lights, with visible red, yellow, and green lenses. The lights are encased in rectangular black housings, each with protective visors above the lenses to shield them from sunlight and rain. The pole is reinforced with horizontal and diagonal support bars connecting the light housings to the central shaft. The overall construction appears robust and weather-resistant, suitable for installation at intersections or road crossings. No pedestrian signals or external control boxes are visible on the structure.
description: A dual-sided, pole-mounted traffic light with vertically stacked red, yellow, and green signals on each side, supported by a black metal pole and a concrete base.
description_view_0: Front view showing both sets of signal lights, each with three visible lenses and protective visors, mounted on a central black pole.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the signal housings and the vertical alignment of the lights, with the pole and base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of both signal housings and the supporting pole structure, with no visible lenses.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the signal housings and the vertical stack of lights, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
